title: 10 best herbal capsules for eczema

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with eczema because they contain the potent anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The capsules provide a concentrated dose of aloe's natural healing agents, penetrating deep into the skin to reduce redness and itching. Regular use can also help to repair and strengthen the skin's barrier function, reducing inflammation and promoting a more balanced skin environment. This natural approach can provide long-term relief from eczema symptoms and promote healthy, clear skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema, providing relief from symptoms.

Antioxidants: Aloe vera contains antioxidants such as vitamins C and E, which help to neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from further damage caused by oxidative stress, common in eczema sufferers.
Moisturizing properties: Herbal aloe capsules provide moisture to the skin, helping to soothe dryness and irritation associated with eczema, while also promoting skin hydration and elasticity.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is a anthraquinone compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms by reducing inflammation and protecting the skin from oxidative damage.

Aloe barbadensis polysaccharides: These polysaccharides, also known as mucopolysaccharides, have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.

Vitamins A, C, and E: These antioxidant vitamins can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, promote skin healing, and reduce inflammation, making them beneficial for eczema sufferers.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from aloe vera leaves is commonly used in capsules for eczema due to its soothing and moisturizing properties.
Gel from Leaves: The inner gel of aloe vera leaves is another key component, rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help alleviate eczema symptoms.
Aloe Vera Juice (from Leaves): Aloe vera juice, extracted from the leaves, is widely used in capsules to help reduce inflammation, itching, and redness associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh aloe vera gel from 3-4 mature plants.
Grind the aloe vera gel into a fine paste using a blender or grinder.
Mix 10 grams of gelatin powder with 100 ml of distilled water.
Combine the aloe vera paste with the gelatin mixture and stir well.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the aloe vera mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules contain a rich source of antioxidants, flavonoids, and iridoid glycosides that work together to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema. Additionally, the capsules' ability to calm the immune system and reduce histamine production helps alleviate symptoms such as redness, swelling, and skin lesions. By taking stinging nettle capsules regularly, individuals may experience a significant reduction in their eczema symptoms and improved overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant activity: Nettle capsules have antioxidant properties that neutralize free radicals, preventing skin damage and oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Vitamin and mineral content: Stinging nettle is a rich source of vitamins A, C, E, K, and B-complex, as well as minerals like calcium, magnesium, and potassium, which support skin health and reduce the risk of eczema flares.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: They help with eczema by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce the severity of symptoms and promote skin healing.
Flavonoids: They help with eczema by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the skin from damage and reduce inflammation.
Sterols (particularly beta-sitosterol): They help with eczema by having anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce skin inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as histamine and flavonoids, which help soothe and reduce eczema symptoms.
Stems: Contain similar properties to leaves, including antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which aid in reducing inflammation and promoting skin health.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the harvested urtica dioica leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 2 grams of the powdered urtica dioica leaves with 1 gram of vegetable cellulose powder in a small bowl.
Use a capsule filler machine to fill empty vegetable capsules with the urtica dioica and cellulose powder mixture.
Label and store the filled urtica dioica capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with eczema because they contain allantoin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and calms irritated skin. The herb's mucilage also forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and reducing dryness and itchiness. Additionally, comfrey has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote tissue repair, making it an effective natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms such as redness, itching, and rashes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Comfrey contains compounds like allantoin, rosmarinic acid, and caffeic acid that reduce inflammation, which helps to soothe and calm itchy skin associated with eczema.

Wound-healing and tissue-regenerating properties: The plant's bioactive compounds stimulate collagen synthesis, promote blood vessel growth, and accelerate wound closure, helping to repair damaged skin and reduce the appearance of eczema lesions.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Comfrey's antioxidant flavonoids and phenolic acids neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema symptoms, while its antimicrobial compounds help prevent infection and promote a healthy skin microbiome.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: An uronic acid derivative that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration.

Flavonoids (e.g., kaempferol, quercetin): Plant-based antioxidants that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of eczema such as redness, itching, and swelling.
Saponins (e.g., syringaldehyde): Compounds with surfactant properties that can help to reduce skin irritation, promote skin hydration, and improve the skin's natural barrier function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of mucilages, which help soothe and moisturize the skin, providing relief from eczema symptoms.
Roots: Roots are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Stems: Stems, particularly the underground stems or rhizomes, are used for their antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage and promote healing in eczema sufferers.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 grams of dried symphytum officinale roots and leaves from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Dry the harvested material at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 30 seconds.
Fill gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the powdered symphytum officinale using a capsule filler machine.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with eczema because they contain high amounts of antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals that soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of dandelion help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial properties prevent bacterial infections from worsening symptoms. Additionally, dandelion's ability to promote digestive health can also alleviate eczema triggers such as food sensitivities. By addressing these underlying causes, dandelion capsules provide a natural and effective way to manage eczema symptoms and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The bioactive compounds in dandelion, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, have been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Soil-purifying effects: Dandelion's roots are known for their ability to purify the blood by removing toxins and heavy metals, which can contribute to eczema symptoms and improve skin health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Taraxacum officinale have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of eczema symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting skin healing.
Saponins: Saponins in Taraxacum officinale have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties, which can help alleviate eczema symptoms such as itching, redness, and swelling.

Phenolic acids (such as ferulic acid): Phenolic acids like ferulic acid in Taraxacum officinale have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage, promoting a healthy skin barrier and reducing eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and anti-itching properties, which help to alleviate eczema symptoms and promote skin comfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect 1 pound of taraxacum officinale roots and leaves, clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Dry the taraxacum officinale in a dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 8 hours or air dry for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 ounces of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part of the taraxacum officinale powder with 2 parts of a filler such as potato starch or cellulose.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with eczema because they contain flavonoids and carotenoids that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with the condition. The capsules also contain saponins, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy barrier function. Additionally, pot marigold has been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory cytokines, further contributing to its beneficial effects on eczema symptoms. By addressing these underlying causes, pot marigold capsules can provide effective relief for individuals suffering from eczema.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in pot marigold capsules neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from oxidative damage caused by environmental stressors, pollutants, and UV radiation, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Soothing and moisturizing effects: The mucilages and saponins found in pot marigold help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing dryness and discomfort associated with eczema, while also providing natural hydration and protection to the skin barrier.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with eczema by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Triterpenoids (specifically, beta-sitosterol): Act as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant agents, reducing redness and swelling in eczema-prone skin, while also promoting wound healing and skin regeneration.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): Possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of eczema symptoms, such as itching and skin redness, by modulating immune responses and protecting against oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are primarily used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are used to create a more comprehensive herbal remedy, as they are believed to possess additional therapeutic benefits when combined with flowers.

Seeds: Seeds are occasionally used as they may contain some of the same beneficial compounds as the flowers, although their use is less prevalent due to their lower concentration of active ingredients.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 calendula officinalis flowers on a sunny day for optimal potency and freshness.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer at 105f for 2 hours to prevent over drying.
Use a coffee grinder to grind 2 tablespoons of dried flowers into a fine powder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the powder with 2 tablespoons of gelatin to create a thick paste.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the paste and seal them immediately to preserve the potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with eczema because they contain hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema, while also soothing irritated skin. Additionally, St John's Wort has been shown to modulate the immune system, which can help to prevent flare-ups caused by an overactive response to allergens or irritants. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin function, St John's Wort capsules can provide relief from the symptoms of eczema, allowing individuals to enjoy clear and comfortable skin.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Flavonoids: The flavonoid content in St. John's Wort capsules may help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema.
Chlorogenic acid: This compound has been shown to possess antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin that can contribute to eczema flare-ups.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress associated with eczema.
Hypericin: This naphthodianthron derivative has phototoxic properties, but it also exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help soothe and calm eczema symptoms.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of eczema, such as redness, itching, and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Hypericum perforatum contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Flowers: The flowers of Hypericum perforatum contain hyperforin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eczema symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Hypericum perforatum contain hypericin, a compound with antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm eczema-prone skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried flowers of the specified plant at the end of summer or in early fall.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area for at least 2 weeks.
Crush the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Fill gelatin capsules with 300-500 milligrams of the powdered plant material and seal the capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower capsules helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe itchy skin and reduce redness. The capsule's active ingredient, echinacea, has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and preventing flare-ups. Additionally, Kansas coneflower capsules have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from further damage, promoting a faster healing process and improving overall skin health. By using these capsules regularly, individuals with eczema can experience a significant reduction in symptoms and improved skin quality.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and lignans, which can help reduce inflammation in the skin, alleviating symptoms of eczema.

Antioxidant activity: Herbal Kansas Coneflower Capsules have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development and exacerbation of eczema.

Immune system modulation: The capsules may modulate the immune system's response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a healthy balance between the Th1 and Th2 immune responses, which is important for managing eczema.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.

Alkaloids (mainly cichoric acid and echinacoside): These compounds have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation in eczema-prone skin.

Caffeic acid derivatives (mainly chicoric acid): These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the skin in eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea root is commonly used due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of eczema.
Leaves: Echinacea leaves are used because they contain compounds that may help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with eczema.
Roots: Echinacea roots are also used due to their ability to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off underlying infections that may contribute to eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 3-4 ounces of dried root of echinacea angustifolia, dried to a moisture content of 10%.
Grind the root into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift the ground root through a 40-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Mix 2000 mg of the sifted powder with 100 mg of filler material, such as cellulose, in a clean bowl.
Fill 100-count gelatin capsules with 20 mg of the powder mixture each and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut capsules helps with eczema because of their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The capsules contain aescin, a bioactive compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema. Aescin also improves skin elasticity and reduces oxidative stress, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms. Additionally, horse chestnut extracts have been found to inhibit the migration of immune cells that contribute to allergic reactions, providing relief from itchy, irritated skin. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin function, horse chestnut capsules offer a natural remedy for managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and reduce inflammation that exacerbates eczema.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in horse chestnut have anti-allergic and anti-pruritic properties, which help to alleviate itching, redness, and irritation associated with eczema by blocking the release of histamine and other allergic mediators.
remedy_constituents: Aescin: Aescin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that may help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
Isoorientin: Isoorientin, a flavonoid glycoside, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce skin inflammation and promote skin healing, alleviating eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Buds: Used to make capsules for eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Seeds: Used to make capsules for eczema due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
Leaves: Used to make capsules for eczema due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_recipe: Grind 1 part dried aesculus hippocastanum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powder with 2 parts cellulose or gelatin and add a small amount of water to form a uniform dough in 20 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill the powder mixture into empty capsules in 10-15 minutes.
Label the capsules with the ingredients and dosage information and store them in a cool dry place for up to 1 year.
Store the filled capsules in a container with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place for up to 1 year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with eczema because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This natural ingredient can reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema flare-ups. Additionally, glycyrrhizin helps to soothe the skin, promoting hydration and elasticity. As a result, herbal licorice capsules can provide relief from itchy, dry skin and help to calm irritated patches of eczema, allowing for faster healing and reduced symptoms over time.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling associated with eczema.
Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties that help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of eczema.

Liquiritin: This flavonoid glycoside has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antipruritic (anti-itching) effects, making it a valuable component in helping to relieve the symptoms of eczema such as itching and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and immunosuppressive properties, which help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.

Flavonoids (particularly Liquiritin and Iso-Liquiritin): These phenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.

Licoricidin: This isoflavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help to reduce itching, redness, and swelling associated with eczema, making it an effective constituent in managing eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for eczema because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are not typically used in capsule form, however, some herbal supplements may contain leaf extracts, but this is less common than root extracts.
Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make capsules for eczema because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the glycyrrhiza glabra roots in a low temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to remove moisture content.
Grind the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 200 rpm.
Mix 500 mg of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 50 mg of starch and 20 mg of cellulose.
Fill the mixture into empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler at 50 capsules per minute.
Store the herbal glycyrrhiza glabra capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with eczema because they contain mucilage, a protective coating that soothes and calms irritated skin. When taken internally, the mucilage absorbs into the bloodstream and reaches the skin, where it forms a barrier against moisture loss and reduces inflammation. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as dryness, itching, and redness, providing relief from eczema flare-ups. Additionally, marshmallow capsules may also help to repair damaged skin tissue, promoting healthier and more resilient skin over time.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness associated with eczema.

Emollient: The gel-like substance in marshmallow root forms a protective barrier on the skin's surface, locking in moisture and providing long-lasting relief from dryness and irritation common in eczema.

Antioxidant: Marshmallow root is rich in antioxidants, which help combat oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healing of skin lesions characteristic of eczema.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eczema.
Gallic acid: Gallic acid exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling in eczema-prone skin.

Irridoid glycosides (specifically, 11,19-dihydroxy-19-acetyl-11,17-dioxo-1,2-didehydroandrographolide): These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the body's immune response and alleviate eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are used due to their rich content of mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties to help alleviate eczema symptoms.
Stems: Stems contain mucilages, similar to roots, which provide relief and comfort to dry, itchy skin associated with eczema.
Leaves: Leaves are used due to their mucilage content, which helps to calm and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis roots from a reputable supplier or grow them yourself in a controlled environment.
Dry the harvested roots in a single layer on paper towels at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent such as gelatin or hydroxypropyl methylcellulose in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using an encapsulation machine or a manual capsule filler for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with eczema because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can effectively calm and reduce skin irritation. The antioxidants present in chamomile help to reduce inflammation and prevent further damage to the skin, promoting a healthy and calm complexion. Additionally, chamomile's natural antihistamine properties can alleviate itching and scratching, providing relief from eczema symptoms. As a result, herbal chamomile capsules can be an effective natural remedy for managing eczema and improving overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile contains apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant activity: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema symptoms and slow down healing processes.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and itching, and promoting relaxation, which is essential for managing eczema flare-ups.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eczema.
Flowers: Containing apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce redness and itching caused by eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at peak bloom in the morning when dew is still present.
Dry the harvested flowers using a dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or air dry them for several days.
Grind dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 400mg of ground matricaria chamomilla with 100mg of gelatin capsules to fill the desired number of capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur capsules helps with eczema because they contain petasin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This compound works by reducing the release of histamine, a chemical responsible for triggering allergic reactions and itching. By decreasing histamine levels, butterbur capsules can help alleviate symptoms of eczema such as redness, swelling, and itching. Additionally, petasin may also help to reduce the severity of eczema flare-ups and promote healthy skin by calming down inflammation and soothing dryness.
remedy_properties: Butanolic extract: This compound has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Carnosic acid: This antioxidant-rich compound helps to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate eczema symptoms.
Iso-butylamides: These natural compounds have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical mediator involved in allergic reactions and itching associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the butterbur plant contain potent bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties, making them effective in soothing and reducing itching associated with eczema.
Buds: Butterbur buds are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-allergic and anti-inflammatory activities, helping to alleviate the symptoms of eczema such as redness and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried petasites hybridus leaf and flowers from a reputable supplier or your own cultivated source.
Grind the 100g of dried petasites hybridus into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 40g of the powdered petasites hybridus with 60g of vitamin e oil in a clean glass jar for 5 minutes.
Fill 50 empty vegetarian capsules with 200mg each of the petasites hybridus-vitamin e mixture and seal tightly immediately after filling.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry capsules helps with eczema because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated skin. The berries contain a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress. These natural compounds also have antioxidant effects, which protect the skin from further damage and promote healing. Additionally, highbush cranberry capsules may help to modulate the immune response, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a healthy skin barrier function.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common triggers for eczema flare-ups.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making it effective in reducing itching and redness associated with eczema.
Proanthocyanidins: These bioactive compounds have been shown to improve skin barrier function and reduce water loss, helping to soothe dry, itchy skin common in eczema sufferers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing eczema symptoms.
Fruits: Rich in antioxidants and anthocyanins, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties and may help reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100g of fresh vaccinium macrocarpon leaves and stems in a well-ventilated area for 2-3 weeks.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 20g of the powdered material with 10g of glycerin and 80g of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container for 30 minutes.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 2 hours before filtering it through a coffee filter to create a liquid extract.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500mg of the liquid extract and seal immediately for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mongolian dandelion capsules helps with eczema because they contain bioactive compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The extracts from the plant's leaves and roots help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and itching associated with eczema. Additionally, Mongolian dandelion has been shown to regulate the immune system, which is often compromised in individuals with eczema, allowing for a healthier balance of skin cells and tissues. As a result, herbal Mongolian dandelion capsules can provide effective relief from eczema symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mongolian Dandelion extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a major contributor to eczema symptoms such as redness and swelling.
Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in Mongolian Dandelion capsules help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eczema.
Immunomodulatory effects: Mongolian Dandelion extracts have been found to modulate the immune response, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines that can contribute to eczema symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Mongolian dandelion contain bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming eczema symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the Mongolian dandelion have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like eczema due to their high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh taraxacum mongolicum roots and dry them in a shaded area for at least 2 weeks.
Grind 30g of dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 15 minutes.
Mix the powdered root with 150ml of boiling water to create a decoction that simmers for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and collect 100ml of liquid extract within 10 minutes.
Fill gelatin capsules with 200mg of the taraxacum mongolicum root extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with eczema because of their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The herb eyebright, also known as Euphrasia officinalis, contains compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. When taken in capsule form, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and reach affected skin areas, providing relief from itching, irritation, and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of skin flora, eyebright capsules can help to alleviate symptoms of eczema, promoting clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Eyebright contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Eyebright capsules can neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from oxidative stress, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting skin health.

Softpith-protecting mucilages: The mucilage content in Eyebright capsules helps to soothe and protect the soft tissues of the skin, including the epidermis and dermis, which can become compromised during eczema flares.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of eyebright contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated skin, making them ideal for treating eczema.
Rhyzomes: Eyebright's rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like eczema due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried euphrasia officinalis leaves and flowers in quantities sufficient for your needs.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix one part powdered euphrasia officinalis with two parts gelatin to create a capsule filler.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared mixture, ensuring each capsule contains 200-500 milligrams of herb.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with eczema because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema. The active ingredients in fennel capsules, such as anethole and fenchone, also have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine, a chemical that triggers allergic reactions and exacerbates eczema symptoms. This natural approach can provide relief from uncomfortable and painful skin conditions without harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of eczema.
Antioxidant properties: Fennel's antioxidant compounds neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to eczema symptoms like itching, redness, and scaling.

Soothing and calming effects: Fennel's essential oils, particularly anethole, have a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with eczema, while also promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eczema.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain anethole, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds and dry them thoroughly for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the foeniculum vulgare powder with 1000 milliliters of distilled water to create a decoction.
Simmer the mixture for 30 minutes at a gentle boil before straining it through a coffee filter or cheesecloth.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the cooled and strained liquid extract using an encapsulation machine or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with eczema because they contain flavonoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with eczema. The capsules also rich in antioxidants that combat free radicals, which can contribute to skin irritation and inflammation. Additionally, hawthorn's antihistaminic effects can alleviate itching and discomfort, allowing the skin to heal more effectively. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy skin cell function, hawthorn capsules can help to alleviate eczema symptoms and promote clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Hawthorn capsules contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and rutin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in hawthorn, including vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage and inflammation, promoting healing and reducing the severity of eczema symptoms.

Immunomodulatory effects: Hawthorn's immunomodulatory compounds may help regulate the immune response, which can reduce excessive inflammatory reactions that exacerbate eczema and promote a more balanced immune system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots are a rich source of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in soothing and calming eczema symptoms.
Barks: The barks of hawthorn contain flavonoids and polyphenols that help reduce inflammation and relieve itching associated with eczema.
remedy_recipe: Harvest crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers in the summer months for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material in a warm, dark place for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind 20 grams of dried crataegus monogyna into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 50 grams of silica gel and 10 grams of vitamin e oil in a clean glass jar for 24 hours to enhance absorption.
Fill 500 milligram capsules with the herbal mixture and seal immediately to preserve potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with eczema because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds that soothe and calm irritated skin. The capsules' unique blend of herbal extracts, such as elderflower and calendula, work together to reduce inflammation and redness, while also promoting healthy skin barrier function. Additionally, the antioxidants in elder capsules help protect the skin from environmental stressors and damage, reducing the severity of eczema symptoms and improving overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to eczema symptoms such as redness, itching, and swelling.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in elder capsules have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help calm the immune response that exacerbates eczema and reduce the severity of symptoms.
Sambunigrin: This saponin compound has been shown to exhibit antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate itching and reduce inflammation associated with eczema.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and triterpenes, which help reduce inflammation and itching associated with eczema.
Barks: Contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making them effective in soothing eczema symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 35 degrees celsius for 6-8 hours or until crispy.
Grind 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part ground sambucus nigra with 2 parts gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a small bowl.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture, filling each one to about 3/4 full before sealing with a dropper cap for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with eczema because they provide a natural anti-inflammatory response, soothing itchy skin and reducing redness. The plantain extract contained in the capsules has been shown to calm inflammation and irritation, providing relief from the constant itching and discomfort associated with eczema. Additionally, plantain's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin, allowing for a faster recovery and reduced risk of flare-ups.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Plantain contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with eczema.
Antioxidant activity: Plantain's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that exacerbate eczema symptoms.

Wound-healing agents: Plantain has been traditionally used to promote wound healing, which is beneficial for eczema patients who often experience skin lesions and cracks; its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties can help facilitate the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Plantain roots have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions like eczema due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal capsules.
Barks: The barks of the plantain plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, making them a popular choice for eczema treatments.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh plantago major leaves and dry them using air circulation or dehydrator at 60°c for 2 hours.
Grind dried plantago major leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Sift the powdered plantago major through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Mix 500mg of powdered plantago major with 250mg of glycerin and 50mg of vitamin e oil in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eczema are natural supplements made from various plants and herbs that have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and soothing properties. These capsules help to reduce the severity of eczema symptoms such as itching, redness, and dryness by promoting skin hydration and calming inflammation. Examples of herbal capsules that can help with eczema include turmeric, chamomile, and calendula. By taking these supplements, individuals can experience significant relief from eczema symptoms, allowing them to enjoy improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and increased confidence in their daily lives.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eczema that are treated with herbal capsules are atopic dermatitis, psoriasis, and acne. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like turmeric, ginger, and chamomile can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and itching associated with these conditions. Other herbs such as stinging nettle and red clover may also be used to reduce skin irritation and promote healing.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eczema is Turmeric, Aloe Vera, and Licorice Root. Turmeric contains curcumin, which reduces inflammation and soothes skin irritations. Aloe Vera has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties, which help to calm and hydrate the skin. Licorice Root, also known as Glycyrrhiza Glabra, reduces redness and swelling, while also providing antioxidant benefits. Taking these capsules together may help to alleviate eczema symptoms, such as itching and dryness, and promote overall skin health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Holistic nursing practice, the use of herbal capsules, specifically chamomile preparation, has shown to be as effective as hydrocortisone in relieving symptoms of itching and inflammation associated with eczema. The study's integrative treatment protocol highlights the importance of addressing essential fatty acid deficiency and food allergies as primary causative factors of eczema. By using a patented chamomile preparation, individuals with eczema can experience enhanced granulation and epithelialization, reducing the need for corticosteroid therapy and its associated side effects.
